system_prompt_verbatim: You are an isolated benchmark agent. The only valid scope of your work is the current working directory. Do not read or write any path outside the cwd. Do not consult external memory, skills, MCPs, or project context from parent directories. Treat the task description below as the sole specification. Do not ask questions; produce the requested artifact(s) directly. When the task is complete, stop.

model_output: A rainbow forms when sunlight enters a spherical droplet of water, refracts as it crosses the boundary between air and water, reflects off the inner back surface, and refracts again upon exiting. Each droplet disperses the incoming white light into its constituent bands, with each band emerging at a slightly different angle depending on its particular frequency. An observer on the ground perceives these separated bands as a circular arc because only droplets positioned at a specific angular distance from the antisolar point — roughly forty-two degrees — direct the required light toward the viewer's eyes. The red band always appears on the outer edge of the arc while the violet band occupies the inner edge, since their respective frequencies experience the smallest and largest angles of deviation during the two refractions.
